Bug Key: OCPBUGS-81323
Component: Etcd
Priority: Major
Issue Type: Bug
Summary: The etcd-operator incorrectly reports healthy etcd members as unhealthy due to API server throttling, causing false positive cluster degradation alerts.
Description: **Description of problem:**

```
ClusterOperator etcd: Reports "Degraded=True, Available=False"
Actual etcd status: All 3 members healthy, leader election working, data synchronized
jtaleric at draco in ~/code/tinker on main* $ oc exec -n openshift-etcd etcd-ip-10-0-64-226.us-west-2.compute.internal -c etcd -- sh -c 'time etcdctl endpoint health --write-out=table'; oc get co/etcd
+--------------------------+--------+------------+-------+
|         ENDPOINT         | HEALTH |    TOOK    | ERROR |
+--------------------------+--------+------------+-------+
| https://10.0.64.226:2379 |   true | 8.247882ms |       |
|   https://10.0.22.9:2379 |   true |  10.4248ms |       |
|   https://10.0.56.9:2379 |   true |  10.6166ms |       |
+--------------------------+--------+------------+-------+

real	0m0.023s
user	0m0.014s
sys	0m0.014s
NAME   VERSION                              AVAILABLE   PROGRESSING   DEGRADED   SINCE   MESSAGE
etcd   4.22.0-0.nightly-2026-03-23-022245   False       False

Bug Key: OCPBUGS-81146
Component: kube-apiserver
Priority: Undefined
Issue Type: Bug
Summary: 4.20 - Extreme CPU Usage observed from Kubernetes components due to golang-fips TLS1.3 handling
Description: **Description of problem:**

```
Customer is seeing huge CPU usage on the kube-apiserver after upgrading to OCP 4.20.

Customer managed to get a pprof from an API server as this occurred 
and found that 60%+ of the time was being spent handling TLS handshakes with the following call tree:

runtime.cgocall                                    164.63s  61.05%
  crypto/tls.(*Conn).HandshakeContext              161.88s  60.03%
    crypto/tls.(*serverHandshakeStateTLS13).handshake  148.86s  55.20%
      crypto/tls.expandLabel                       154.47s  57.28%
        io.ReadAll                                 153.35s  56.86%
          golang-fips/openssl/v2.(*hkdf).Read
            _Cfunc_go_openssl_EVP_PKEY_derive_wrapper 

Customer noticed that compiling the API server using https://github.com/golang-fips/go and 
believes the root cause of the issues is due the expandLabel handling in there, specifically 
this looks to be very expensive: https://github.com/golang-fips/go/blob/f9990dae1a

Bug Key: OCPBUGS-81124
Component: Etcd
Priority: Undefined
Issue Type: Bug
Summary: Clusters born in OpenShift 4.9 and earlier may have duplicate members upon upgrading to 4.21
Description: Clusters which ran 4.9 and earlier at any point may run into <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-0">https://github.com/etcd-io/etcd/issues/20967</custom> 

Therefore we should

* Increase the minimum version required to upgrade to 4.21 to 4.20.15 where we brought in etcd-3.5.26
* Declare a risk on 4.21.z <= the version in which we implemented <custom data-type="emoji" data-id="id-1">:point_up:</custom> which removes update recommendations for clusters that have 4.9 or lower in their history

This bug can be marked verified once the first step is done, but we should not forget to follow up on the second step. Marking this as UpgradeBlocker

If there’s a workaround to remove the duplicate members after the upgrade to 4.21 it would be good to detail that.

Bug Key: OCPBUGS-80958

Priority: Normal
Issue Type: Bug
Summary: Kube-apiserver operator: KubeAPIServer status.nodeStatuses not cleared on Node delete (same-name replacement)
Description: ## Description of problem

Observed on live TNF cluster (2026-03-20): **\`installer-5-master-0\` completed \~18:45Z** on the **old** host, while the **current** Node `master-0` object was **created \~19:14Z** (new UID). **\`nodeStatuses\` still showed \`master-0\` at \`currentRevision: 5\`** with **no installer pod for \`master-0\` after the new Node existed**, while **\`MissingStaticPodControllerDegraded\`** / **\`GuardControllerDegraded\`** reflected **no kube-apiserver static pod** on the new host.

4.22

Always (when same-name control-plane node replacement is performed)

Bug Key: OCPBUGS-80957
Component: Etcd
Priority: Normal
Issue Type: Bug
Summary: CEO: Etcd status.nodeStatuses not cleared on Node delete (same-name replacement)
Description: ## Description of problem

When a **Node** is **deleted** from the API and later **recreated with the same \`nodeName\`** (e.g. `master-0`), **\`etcd.operator.openshift.io/cluster\` \`status.nodeStatuses\` keeps the old row** keyed only by **\`nodeName\`**. CEO then treats the replacement host as **already at \`currentRevision\`**, so the **node installer / static etcd manifest path may not re-run**.

On-disk symptoms include:

* **missing \`/var/lib/etcd/certs.hash\`**
* **Pacemaker \`podman-etcd\` start failures**
* **\`MissingStaticPodControllerDegraded\`** for etcd on that node

Even though **\`nodeStatuses\` still shows both nodes at the latest revision**.

## Version-Release number

4.22

## How reproducible

Always (when same-name control-plane node replacement is performed)

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Deploy TNF (Two-Node with Fencing) cluster
2. Delete a control-plane Node from the API (e.g., `oc delete node master-0`)
3. Reprovision the node with the same name (`master-0`)
4.

etcd: Authorization bypass allows information disclosure and denial of service

etcd is a distributed key-value store for the data of a distributed system. Prior to versions 3.4.42, 3.5.28, and 3.6.9, unauthorized users may bypass authentication or authorization checks and call certain etcd functions in clusters that expose the gRPC API to untrusted or partially trusted clients. In unpatched etcd clusters with etcd auth enabled, unauthorized users are able to call MemberList and learn cluster topology, including member IDs and advertised endpoints; call Alarm, which can be abused for operational disruption or denial of service; use Lease APIs, interfering with TTL-based keys and lease ownership; and/or trigger compaction, permanently removing historical revisions and disrupting watch, audit, and recovery workflows. Bug Key: OCPBUGS-80812
Component: kube-apiserver
Priority: Undefined
Issue Type: Vulnerability
Summary: CVE-2026-33186 openshift4/ose-hyperkube-rhel9: gRPC-Go: Authorization bypass due to improper HTTP/2 path validation [openshift-4.21]
Description: Security Tracking Issue

Do not make this issue public.

Flaw:

---

gRPC-Go: Authorization bypass due to improper HTTP/2 path validation

gRPC-Go is the Go language implementation of gRPC. Versions prior to 1.79.3 have an authorization bypass resulting from improper input validation of the HTTP/2 \`:path\` pseudo-header. The gRPC-Go server was too lenient in its routing logic, accepting requests where the \`:path\` omitted the mandatory leading slash (e.g., \`Service/Method\` instead of \`/Service/Method\`). While the server successfully routed these requests to the correct handler, authorization interceptors (including the official \`grpc/authz\` package) evaluated the raw, non-canonical path string. Consequently, "deny" rules defined using canonical paths (starting with \`/\`) failed to match the incoming request, allowing it to bypass the policy if a fallback "allow" rule was present. This affects gRPC-Go servers that use path-based authorization interceptors, such as the official

Bug Key: OCPBUGS-73780
Component: Installer / openshift-installer
Priority: Major
Issue Type: Bug
Summary: Azure installation fail when v-net subnets are pre-configured.
Description: IPI OCP 4.18.x Installation in Azure fail with error:

```java
ERROR failed to fetch Cluster: failed to generate asset "Cluster": failed to create cluster: failed to create infrastructure manifest: admission webhook "validation.azurecluster.infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io" denied the request: AzureCluster.infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io "csaggin-repro-0419050-jzg58" is invalid: spec.networkSpec.apiServerLB.frontendIPConfigs[0].privateIP: Invalid value: "172.16.0.52": Internal LB IP address needs to be in control plane subnet range ([172.16.0.0/27])
```

The validation fails due to the wrong subnet association when the v-net are already existing.

It is very easy to reproduce:

1) Create the v-net subnet like:

```java
priv-endpoint: 172.16.0.32/29
controlplan:   172.16.0.48/28
workers:       172.16.0.0/27
```

2) Associate the subnet in the install-config.yaml:

```java
networking:
  clusterNetwork:
  - cidr: 10.128.0.0/14
    hostPrefix: 23
  machineNetwork:
  - cidr: 172.16.0.

Description: Description of problem:

```java
The [openshift-kube-scheduler-ip] pod in the [openshift-kube-scheduler] component is using a local, hardcoded TLS configuration. This ticket requires refactoring the component to dynamically inherit its TLS settings from the designated global configuration source, rather than managing them locally.

Summary: ingress-canary DaemonSet no longer honors IngressController tolerations in compact clusters after upgrade to OCP 4.20
Description: Description of problem:

After upgrading from OpenShift 4.19.22 to 4.20.12, the ingress cluster operator reports Degraded=True due to CanaryChecksSucceeding=False. This occurs because the ingress-canary DaemonSet has desired=0 and does not schedule any pods.

The affected environment is a supported compact cluster topology where all nodes are control-plane and unschedulable, with infra labels and taints as documented in KCS 7109585.

In previous versions (4.17–4.19), the DaemonSet correctly inherited tolerations defined in the IngressController.spec.nodePlacement.tolerations section (e.g., { operator: Exists }), allowing pods to run on tainted infra/master nodes.

In 4.20.12, the ingress-canary DaemonSet appears to use only a fixed toleration (key: node-role.kubernetes.io/infra) and no longer respects custom tolerations defined by the user, resulting in no schedulable nodes and ingress operator degradation.

This breaks compatibility with previously working and supported cluster archit

Bug Key: OCPBUGS-63478
Component: Networking / cluster-network-operator
Priority: Normal
Issue Type: Bug
Summary: Component Readiness: pods should successfully create sandboxes by adding pod to network: adds operator Progressing checks to the deleted pod branch 
Description: Description of problem:

```java
For deleted pods, the code never checks if the failure occurred during operator Progressing. It only checks the time difference, which results in a hard failure at > 5 seconds. Bug Key: OCPBUGS-78785
Component: Bare Metal Hardware Provisioning / baremetal-operator
Priority: Undefined
Issue Type: Bug
Summary: Make bootMACAddress optional for virtual media BMC drivers when inspection is enabled
Description: **Description of problem:**

```
  Virtual media BMC drivers (redfish-virtualmedia, idrac-virtualmedia, ilo5-virtualmedia) can boot from virtual media and discover the host's MAC address during hardware
  inspection. Currently, bootMACAddress is required on BareMetalHost resources regardless of the BMC driver type, which forces users to manually determine and provide the boot MAC
  address before creating the BMH.

  This change makes bootMACAddress optional for virtual media drivers when hardware inspection is enabled, since the MAC address can be discovered automatically during the
  inspection process. bootMACAddress remains required when:
  - The BMC driver uses PXE boot (e.g., ipmi, libvirt), which needs the MAC for DHCP/TFTP
  - Inspection is disabled (via InspectionMode field or inspect.metal3.io annotation), since there is no other mechanism to discover the MAC

  Changes include:
  - Update virtual media driver NeedsMAC() methods to return false
  - Add webhook validation to r

Bug Key: OCPBUGS-78763
Component: HyperShift
Priority: Undefined
Issue Type: Bug
Summary: global-pull-secret daemonset node labeling race condition causes pods not to be scheduled on Replace upgrade strategy nodes in time
Description: ## Description of problem

The global-pull-secret daemonset relies on a node label to schedule pods onto nodes. For NodePools using the Replace upgrade strategy, the HCCO globalps controller reactively labels nodes after they appear. This creates a race condition: the node may be ready before the controller labels it, causing the daemonset pod to not be scheduled on that node.

The fix moves the labeling from the HCCO globalps controller to the NodePool controller, propagating the label via CAPI Machine labels so it is applied during node onboarding rather than reactively.

## Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable)

4.22

## How reproducible

Sometimes (race condition — depends on timing between node creation and controller reconciliation)

## Steps to Reproduce

Bug Key: OCPBUGS-78745

Priority: Major
Issue Type: Bug
Summary: [release-4.20] - Cluster Baremetal Operator webhook is preventing IBI workflow to work with o-cloud manager
Description: Description of problem:

```java
The baremetal-operator-validating-webhook-configuration is preventing updates to the BaremetalHost.spec.externallyProvisioned property in existing BMHs.

When using o-cloud manager to provisioning clusters, it requires BaremetaHosts to be inspected before these can 
be provisioned. This prevents users from setting externallyProvisioned: true when adding BMHs to the o-cloud manager inventory.

When using IBI to deploy clusters, it's expected that the BMH has the externallyProvisioned property set to true so the configuration ISO can be attached to the node and at thesame time the node boots from the hard drive.

The SiteConfig operator will try to set the externallyProvisioned property to true if the BMH selected for the cluster provisioning doesn't have it set.

The admission webhook will block changes to the BMH and the cluster won't be deployed.
```

Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):

```java
4.20
```

Description: Description of problem:

```java
The baremetal-operator-validating-webhook-configuration is preventing updates to the BaremetalHost.spec.externallyProvisioned property in existing BMHs.

When using o-cloud manager to provisioning clusters, it requires BaremetaHosts to be inspected before these can 
be provisioned. This prevents users from setting externallyProvisioned: true when adding BMHs to the o-cloud manager inventory.

When using IBI to deploy clusters, it's expected that the BMH has the externallyProvisioned property set to true so the configuration ISO can be attached to the node and at thesame time the node boots from the hard drive.

The SiteConfig operator will try to set the externallyProvisioned property to true if the BMH selected for the cluster provisioning doesn't have it set.

The admission webhook will block changes to the BMH and the cluster won't be deployed.
```

Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):

```java
4.20
```

Bug Key: OCPBUGS-78742
Component: HyperShift
Priority: Normal
Issue Type: Bug
Summary: GlobalPullSecret e2e test flaky due to race condition with CountAvailableNodes during kubelet restarts
Description: This is a clone of issue OCPBUGS-67262. The following is the description of the original issue:   
—  
Description of problem:

```java
The EnsureGlobalPullSecret e2e test is flaky because CountAvailableNodes() is called while nodes are temporarily NotReady due to kubelet restarts triggered by global-pull-secret-syncer.

When additional-pull-secret is updated or deleted, the global-pull-secret-syncer DaemonSet updates /var/lib/kubelet/config.json and restarts kubelet on each node. If CountAvailableNodes() is called during this window, it returns a stale count (e.g., 1 instead of 2).

This causes waitForDaemonSetsReady() to either:
- False pass: actualReady(1) == nodeCount(1) passes prematurely
- False fail: actualReady(2) != nodeCount(1) fails forever with "2/1 pods ready"
```

Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):

```java
HyperShift main branch (affects all versions with GlobalPullSecret e2e test)
```

```java

Bug Key: OCPBUGS-78731
Component: Networking / ovn-kubernetes
Priority: Undefined
Issue Type: Bug
Summary: OVN-K multicast silently disabled: enable-multicast in wrong config section causes gcfg to discard it
Description: ## Description of problem

The OVN-Kubernetes multicast feature is silently disabled at runtime even when the cluster administrator sets \`enable-multicast=true\` in the OVN-K config file. The root cause is a gcfg parser misconfiguration: \`enable-multicast\` is written under the \`\[ovnkubernetesfeature\]\` section in \`ovnkube.conf\`, but \`EnableMulticast\` is a **global** config variable in \`config.go\` — not a field inside the \`OVNKubernetesFeature\` struct. The gcfg parser emits a warning and silently discards the value, leaving \`config.EnableMulticast = false\` at runtime.

As a result, no multicast ACLs are created in the OVN NB database, and all cross-node multicast traffic is dropped (100% packet loss).

**Warning in OVN-K logs:**

```java
W config.go:2724: can't store data at section "ovnkubernetesfeature", variable "enable-multicast"
```

**OVN NB DB symptom (broken cluster):**

* \`clusterRtrPortGroup acls: \[\]\` (empty — no multicast ACLs)
* No \`mcast_snoop\` on node

Bug Key: OCPBUGS-78707
Component: MicroShift
Priority: Critical
Issue Type: Bug
Summary: Feature gate lists can be set when FeatureSet is empty
Description: ## Root cause

Commit `02b1948a8` intentionally removed the validation check.

Before that commit, `validateFeatureGates()` at `pkg/config/apiserver.go:196` had:

```go
case "":
    if len(fg.CustomNoUpgrade.Enabled) > 0 || len(fg.CustomNoUpgrade.Disabled) > 0 {
        return fmt.Errorf("CustomNoUpgrade enabled/disabled lists must be empty when FeatureSet is empty")
    }
    return nil
```

Now it's simply:

```go
case "":
    return nil
```

When `featureSet` is `""` (empty string), validation returns `nil` immediately — **regardless** of what's in the `customNoUpgrade.enabled/disabled` lists.

This means a user can write:

```yaml
featureGates:
  featureSet: ""
  customNoUpgrade:
    enabled: ["SomeFeature"]
```

and MicroShift will:

* Pass validation (no checks run)
* Apply `SomeFeature=true` to kube-apiserver (via `ToApiserverArgs`)
* **Not** create an upgrade lock file
* Allow upgrades that could break the cluster due to the custom feature gate

The old validation was protectin

Summary: HyperShift filtering of CVO manifests should respect feature-set annotation

```

changes for CRD filenames in the openshift/api repository disallows updating the 
openshift/api module in the openshift/cluster-version-operator repository.

Bug Key: OCPBUGS-78702
Component: Cluster Autoscaler
Priority: Critical
Issue Type: Bug
Summary: [4.16] Cluster Autoscaler CAPI provider accumulates replica decrements on paused MachineDeployments causing catastrophic scale-down on unpause
Description: Description of problem:

* When a MachineDeployment's reconciliation is paused (via cluster.x-k8s.io/paused annotation or spec.pausedUntil on HostedCluster), the Cluster Autoscaler continues to identify unneeded nodes and call DeleteNodes(). Each call decrements .spec.replicas on the MachineDeployment via SetSize(replicas - 1). Since the CAPI controller is not reconciling, machines are never actually deleted — the nodes remain Ready with no deletionTimestamp. CAS sees them as unneeded again on the next loop and decrements replicas further. After enough iterations, .spec.replicas reaches minSize (1). When reconciliation resumes (unpause), the CAPI MachineDeployment controller sees .spec.replicas=1 and mass-deletes all machines down to 1, causing a catastrophic scale-down.

* This is the same class of bug as OCPBUGS-60790 but with a different trigger. The fix in OCPBUGS-60790 (upstream PR [https://github.com/kubernetes/autoscaler/pull/8583](https://github.com/kubernetes/autoscaler/pull/8

Bug Key: OCPBUGS-78696
Component: Installer / OpenShift on OpenStack
Priority: Major
Issue Type: Bug
Summary: Cloud Controller Manager pods crash with `Failed to create an OpenStack LoadBalancer client` while installing a OpenStack IPI cluster
Description: Description of problem:

```java
Installing a 4.20 cluster on OpenStack platform via IPI method fails while bootstrapping the cluster. The cloud controller manager pods crash with following error. The CCM pods constantly restart and crash with the following error. This results into credential sync failing.

~~~
F1107 11:03:17.797998       1 openstack.go:355] Failed to create an OpenStack LoadBalancer client: failed to find load-balancer v2 public endpoint for region : No suitable endpoint could be found in the service catalog.
~~~

The master nodes do get created and join the cluster but are stuck in NotReady state. 

The installation works seamlessly with same clouds.yaml file and install config file for a 4.19 cluster, but fails for 4.20 installation. As a workaround the loadbalencer is manually disabled. Appending the fields below gets the cluster installed. 

~~~
[LoadBalancer] 
enabled: false
~~~
```

Bug Key: OCPBUGS-78690
Component: Cluster Autoscaler
Priority: Critical
Issue Type: Bug
Summary: [4.20] Cluster Autoscaler CAPI provider accumulates replica decrements on paused MachineDeployments causing catastrophic scale-down on unpause
Description: Description of problem:

* When a MachineDeployment's reconciliation is paused (via cluster.x-k8s.io/paused annotation or spec.pausedUntil on HostedCluster), the Cluster Autoscaler continues to identify unneeded nodes and call DeleteNodes(). Each call decrements .spec.replicas on the MachineDeployment via SetSize(replicas - 1). Since the CAPI controller is not reconciling, machines are never actually deleted — the nodes remain Ready with no deletionTimestamp. CAS sees them as unneeded again on the next loop and decrements replicas further. After enough iterations, .spec.replicas reaches minSize (1). When reconciliation resumes (unpause), the CAPI MachineDeployment controller sees .spec.replicas=1 and mass-deletes all machines down to 1, causing a catastrophic scale-down.

* This is the same class of bug as OCPBUGS-60790 but with a different trigger. The fix in OCPBUGS-60790 (upstream PR [https://github.com/kubernetes/autoscaler/pull/8583](https://github.com/kubernetes/autoscaler/pull/8

Bug Key: OCPBUGS-78689
Component: Installer / OpenShift on OpenStack
Priority: Major
Issue Type: Bug
Summary: CLONE - Cloud Controller Manager pods crash with `Failed to create an OpenStack LoadBalancer client` while installing a OpenStack IPI cluster
Description: Description of problem:

```java
Installing a 4.20 cluster on OpenStack platform via IPI method fails while bootstrapping the cluster. The cloud controller manager pods crash with following error. The CCM pods constantly restart and crash with the following error. This results into credential sync failing.

~~~
F1107 11:03:17.797998       1 openstack.go:355] Failed to create an OpenStack LoadBalancer client: failed to find load-balancer v2 public endpoint for region : No suitable endpoint could be found in the service catalog.
~~~

The master nodes do get created and join the cluster but are stuck in NotReady state. 

The installation works seamlessly with same clouds.yaml file and install config file for a 4.19 cluster, but fails for 4.20 installation. As a workaround the loadbalencer is manually disabled. Appending the fields below gets the cluster installed. 

~~~
[LoadBalancer] 
enabled: false
~~~
```

  vendor/github.com/opencontainers/runc/libcontainer/utils/utils_unix.go:334:20: undefined: securejoin.MkdirAllHandle

Summary: Feature gate lists can be set when FeatureSet is empty
Description: ## Root cause

Commit `02b1948a8` intentionally removed the validation check.

Before that commit, `validateFeatureGates()` at `pkg/config/apiserver.go:196` had:

```go
case "":
    if len(fg.CustomNoUpgrade.Enabled) > 0 || len(fg.CustomNoUpgrade.Disabled) > 0 {
        return fmt.Errorf("CustomNoUpgrade enabled/disabled lists must be empty when FeatureSet is empty")
    }
    return nil
```

Now it's simply:

```go
case "":
    return nil
```

When `featureSet` is `""` (empty string), validation returns `nil` immediately — **regardless** of what's in the `customNoUpgrade.enabled/disabled` lists.

This means a user can write:

```yaml
featureGates:
  featureSet: ""
  customNoUpgrade:
    enabled: ["SomeFeature"]
```

and MicroShift will:

* Pass validation (no checks run)
* Apply `SomeFeature=true` to kube-apiserver (via `ToApiserverArgs`)
* **Not** create an upgrade lock file
* Allow upgrades that could break the cluster due to the custom feature gate

Description: cryptography Subgroup Attack Due to Missing Subgroup Validation for SECT Curves

cryptography is a package designed to expose cryptographic primitives and recipes to Python developers. Prior to 46.0.5, the public_key_from_numbers (or EllipticCurvePublicNumbers.public_key()), EllipticCurvePublicNumbers.public_key(), load_der_public_key() and load_pem_public_key() functions do not verify that the point belongs to the expected prime-order subgroup of the curve. This missing validation allows an attacker to provide a public key point P from a small-order subgroup. This can lead to security issues in various situations, such as the most commonly used signature verification (ECDSA) and shared key negotiation (ECDH). When the victim computes the shared secret as S = \[victim_private_key\]P via ECDH, this leaks information about victim_private_key mod (small_subgroup_order). ```
OpenShift installer creates MachineConfigPools (both master and worker) without explicitly setting the spec.maxUnavailable field, relying on implicit defaults. This causes Red Hat Insights to flag fresh out-of-the-box (OOTB) clusters with Risk Level 2 warnings, creating confusion for administrators who expect OOTB configurations to comply with Red Hat's own best practices.

Bug Key: OCPBUGS-81281
Component: Networking / openshift-sdn
Priority: Undefined
Issue Type: Bug
Summary: Set Ceph noout Flag During SDN-to-OVN Migration to Prevent Unnecessary Recovery
Description: **Description of problem:**

```
During the planned SDN-to-OVN migration, a rolling reboot of all nodes will be performed. This can trigger unnecessary Ceph recovery operations if not handled properly.

As highlighted in internal communication, clusters running ODF must have the Ceph noout flag set before starting the migration and unset only after all nodes have successfully rejoined and Ceph reports a healthy status.

This step is critical to avoid unnecessary data rebalancing and recovery during node restarts.

Steps to Perform:

Before starting SDN-to-OVN migration, execute:

ceph osd set noout
Proceed with rolling migration/reboots.

After all nodes are back online and cluster status is HEALTH_OK, execute:

ceph osd unset noout
```

Bug Key: OCPBUGS-81023
Component: Machine Config Operator
Priority: Undefined
Issue Type: Bug
Summary: MCS cannot work with newer versions of Ignition
Description: When Ignition requests config from MCS, it passes an Accepts header that explicitly includes only the latest version of config it can accept, e.g.

```
application/vnd.coreos.ignition+json;version=3.5.0, */*;q=0.1
```

Prior to <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-0">https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/MCO-1591</custom> we used to return the latest available version of config if the Accept version was a higher minor version of 3.x.

After that change (in 4.20), we return `400 Bad Request` if we cannot support the _exact_ version identified in the Accepts header. So every time a new version of Ignition is released with support for a new config version, it will be unable to pull config from MCS until MCS is updated to vendor the new release of Ignition.

This happened with [Ignition 2.26](https://github.com/coreos/ignition/blob/main/docs/release-notes.md#ignition-2260-2026-02-17), which introduced config version 3.6.0 (<custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-1">https://redhat.atla

Summary: OVN-Kubernetes: survivor local SB missing remote peer after same-name control-plane replace (east–west broken)
Description: ## Description of problem

When a control-plane node is reprovisioned while in the cluster (e.g., Ironic reprovisioning in baremetal/VM environments), the new chassis-id conflicts with the existing chassis-id in the node object. OVN-Kubernetes cannot override the chassis-id in the node object, preventing recovery and leaving the cluster in a broken state with no east-west pod traffic.

Symptom: After same-name control-plane node replacement, east-west pod traffic fails with **100% packet loss** between pod subnets across nodes, **\`dial tcp <pod-on-peer>:8443: i/o timeout\`** from `kube-apiserver` to aggregated API backends, **503** / flaky **APIService** checks.

4.22

Always (when control-plane node is reprovisioned while in cluster, creating chassis-id conflict)

Bug Key: OCPBUGS-80925
Component: Cluster Version Operator
Priority: Undefined
Issue Type: Bug
Summary: The default service account in the CVO namespace should not have cluster-admin permissions.
Description: **Description of problem:**

```
https://github.com/openshift/cluster-version-operator/pull/1266 adds 
bespoke service accounts to cluster-version-operator- and version- pods.
However, it also revealed a bug in the CVO logic; using delete annotation
`"release.openshift.io/delete": "true"` does not in fact delete artifacts
after upgrades. This has left us with no choice but to keep a ClusterRoleBinding
for default service account to give it cluster-admin permissions. 

The mentioned pods above will not be using default service account in 4.22+ , 
but the CRB has to remain in order to allow upgrade tests to run successfully. 

This means, however, that in 4.22+ , the default service account in
cluster-version-operator namespace will have cluster-admin permissions in the
core payload, even if no current pods are using it.

          golang-fips/openssl/v2.(*hkdf).Read
            _Cfunc_go_openssl_EVP_PKEY_derive_wrapper

    * On the ocp-tst2 cluster we have 2 rules for port 6441-6442 that allows traffic between workers and the control plane.
    
* But as per OVN upstream [Documentation](https://docs.ovn.org/en/latest/howto/firewalld.html)  6641-6642 is ovn northbound and southbound databases ports and the cluster show different port rules which are conflicting to documentation.

Description: **Description of problem:**

Pods gets stuck in podinitializing state and endpoint is stuck in notReadyAddresses but after deleting the endpoint the pod comes back up

* While the pods are being injected with consul
* As Per Consul:  

    ```
    Although the visible error appears in the Consul connect-init container (Unable to find registered services; retrying), the behavior suggests a timing issue around how the Kubernetes Service/Endpoints state is observed during pod startup.
    What we’re seeing:
     
    •	The Pod schedules successfully.
    •	The Service exists.
    •	During connect-init execution, the injector does not observe the expected service endpoint.
    •	Deleting or recreating the Endpoints object consistently resolves the issue without changing the Pod or Consul configuration.
     
    This indicates that the correct endpoint state can be computed, but in some cases it is not observed by the injector during the initial reconciliation window.
    ```

Description of problem:

```java
When a custom serviceAccountIssuer is set on the Authentication CR, the service-account-jwks-uri argument in the KubeAPIServer CR will no longer be set to https://<API_URL>/openid/v1/jwks. 

This means that the jwks_uri field from the /.well-known/openid-configurations endpoint will now be set to https://<MASTER_NODE_IP>:6443/openid/v1/jwks. The problem is that even if the client machine is configured to trust the master node's certificate CA, there will still be an error because the certificate's SAN does not include the master node's IP.
```

Priority: Normal
Issue Type: Bug
Summary: Cluster-version operator should ignore data-plane proxy configuration on HyperShift
Description: **Description of problem**

Since 4.14, HostedCluster has had [_spec.configuration.proxy_ to configure the _data-plane_ egress](https://github.com/openshift/hypershift/blob/4585fa11d8263470dd3e51d5ccfe3a27ac3e3a4e/api/hypershift/v1beta1/hostedcluster_types.go#L2288-L2295).  However, the cluster-version operator running on the management-cluster control plane has assumed that hosted-cluster Proxy resource should configure its own egress as well.  I’m not sure how folks expect management-cluster-side control-plane egress to be configured, but we don’t want it to respect _spec.configuration.proxy_, because management-cluster networking and data-plane-compute networking are very different environments.

Summary: OCP4.18 - Upgrade step-through from 4.14 to 4.18 each time resulted in degraded node state caused by stalled resolv-prepender (wait-for-br-ex-up) requiring manual correction
Description: **Description of problem:**

During a migration step-through process from 4.14 to 4.18 at each upgrade version master nodes would not return to ready until the service wait-for-br-ex-up was manually stopped, which allowed continued boot to kubelet and crio READY states. 

After a review of the journal on the nodes, it was revealed that the service never completed because we never successfully reached the resolv-prepend exit condition that would create the required file. As a result, the node stalled until we bypassed the sleep condition.

However, the conditional required resolv-prepend task failed to complete in 30s regardless, leading to exit check state.

Component: kube-apiserver
Priority: Normal
Issue Type: Bug
Summary: 4.20- Custom serviceAccountIssuer unsets service-account-jwks-uri
Description: Description of problem:

```java
When a custom serviceAccountIssuer is set on the Authentication CR, the service-account-jwks-uri argument in the KubeAPIServer CR will no longer be set to https://<API_URL>/openid/v1/jwks. 

This means that the jwks_uri field from the /.well-known/openid-configurations endpoint will now be set to https://<MASTER_NODE_IP>:6443/openid/v1/jwks. The problem is that even if the client machine is configured to trust the master node's certificate CA, there will still be an error because the certificate's SAN does not include the master node's IP.
```

Requesting amendments to OpenShift/RHCOS 4.19 documentation to highlight the above change, impact, and recommendation to adjust node monitoring for this specific scenario.

Bug Key: OCPBUGS-79530
Component: HyperShift
Priority: Normal
Issue Type: Bug
Summary: Ignition server may fail to set ignition-reached annotation on token secret due to unretried conflict
Description: Description of problem:

```
The ignition server HTTP handler sets the ignition-reached=True annotation on the
token secret after serving the ignition payload to a node. This annotation is how
the NodePool controller determines that the ReachedIgnitionEndpoint condition should
be True.

The Get+Update used to set this annotation has no retry-on-conflict logic. The
TokenSecretReconciler, running in the same process, may concurrently patch the same
token secret with payload data, which could bump the resourceVersion and cause the
handler's Update to fail with a 409 Conflict. This error is silently logged with no
retry, potentially losing the annotation.

InPlace upgrade NodePools are more susceptible because:
- The TokenSecretReconciler writes compressed payload data into the token secret
- Nodes contact the ignition endpoint exactly once during bootstrap (no reboots)
- Replace/Rolling nodes contact the endpoint multiple times, providing additional
  opportunities for the annotation to s

Bug Key: OCPBUGS-79499
Component: Documentation / Release Notes + Errata
Priority: Undefined
Issue Type: Bug
Summary: driver-toolkit v4.21.7 container image switch of microdnf breaks builds that rely on "dnf install $REMOTE_RPM_URL"
Description: With the release of driver-toolkit v4.21.7, it migrated from `dnf `→ `microdnf` for package management.  This broke a container build workflow that a partner relied on to download and install the EPEL repo rpm from a remote url due to a limitation in \`microdnf\` v3.9 not supporting standalone installation of RPMs (remote or local).    

```shell
# This works in DTK 4.21.6 but breaks in 4.21.7
$ wget https://dl.fedoraproject.org/pub/epel/epel-release-latest-9.noarch.rpm && dnf install -y epel-release-latest-9.noarch.rpm
$ dnf config-manager --add-repo=https://mirror.stream.centos.org/9-stream/AppStream/x86_64/os/
```

  
I don’t feel this qualifies as an actual bug but it feels like something we should be aware of for additional workflows that could be affected.

Bug Key: OCPBUGS-79467
Component: Networking / router
Priority: Major
Issue Type: Bug
Summary: CIO runs at DEBUG log level by default, causing excessive log volume with Sail Library integration
Description: **Description of problem:**

```
The cluster-ingress-operator uses zap.NewDevelopment() which sets the default log level to DEBUG. This causes excessive log volume, particularly problematic with the Sail Library integration for Gateway API which generates approximately 2,000 debug logs per hour during normal operation.

Debug-level logs from the Sail Library include drift detection events, reconciliation triggers, and resource watching that are not relevant for production use and create significant noise in log aggregation systems.
```

**Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):**

```
4.22
```

**How reproducible:**

```
Always
```

**Steps to Reproduce:**

```
1. Deploy cluster-ingress-operator with Gateway API feature enabled (TechPreview or using Sail Library)
2. Create a GatewayClass using openshift-default controller

Summary: RendezvousIP should not be inferred in unconfigured ignition

Bug Key: OCPBUGS-80969
Component: Image Registry
Priority: Normal
Issue Type: Bug
Summary: Migrate image to image gallery
Description: **Description of problem:**

```
Hello,

We opened case 04039641 earlier because, after migrating to federated ID, Openshift could not access the OS image stored in the cluster's RG for clusters created before the image gallery was introduced. A fix was implemented to update the identity permissions and allow access to the image, but the migration from static image to image gallery did not occur.

Bug Key: OCPBUGS-80966
Component: Networking / router
Priority: Minor
Issue Type: Bug
Summary: Ingress operator produces malformed PEM bundle when tls.crt lacks trailing newline
Description: ## Description

PR: [https://github.com/openshift/cluster-ingress-operator/pull/1405](https://github.com/openshift/cluster-ingress-operator/pull/1405) 

The certificate publisher in `cluster-ingress-operator` concatenates `tls.crt` and `tls.key` using `bytes.Join` with a `nil` separator when assembling the `router-certs` secret in `openshift-config-managed`. When `tls.crt` does not end with a trailing newline (which is valid PEM and can happen with certificates produced by certain tools), the resulting bundle has the `END CERTIFICATE` and `BEGIN PRIVATE KEY` markers concatenated on the same line:

```
-----END CERTIFICATE----------BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----
```

This makes the entire PEM bundle unparseable.

## Impact

The `cluster-authentication-operator` copies this secret via `SyncPartialSecret` into `v4-0-config-system-router-certs` in `openshift-authentication`, then attempts to parse it with `crypto.CertsFromPEM`. The malformed PEM causes the authentication operator to go degraded wi

Bug Key: OCPBUGS-78555
Component: Networking / router
Priority: Undefined
Issue Type: Bug
Summary: E2E: assertGatewaySuccessful timeout too short for load balancer provisioning (3m vs 5m)
Description: **Description of problem**

Gateway API E2E tests are flaky because the timeout in `assertGatewaySuccessful` is too short for cloud load balancer provisioning.

Currently, `assertGatewaySuccessful` only waits [3 minutes](https://github.com/openshift/cluster-ingress-operator/blob/master/test/e2e/util_gatewayapi_test.go#L641) for the Gateway to become successful. This is often not enough time for cloud load balancers to provision and for the Gateway status conditions to reflect success, especially under transient control-plane or cloud slowness.

By contrast, for ingress controllers we already always wait at least 5 minutes for `LoadBalancer`-type Services in `waitForIngressControllerCondition`. Gateway API E2E should be consistent with this behavior so that Gateway tests are not stricter than our existing ingress tests.

Bug Key: OCPBUGS-78523
Component: Networking / router
Priority: Normal
Issue Type: Bug
Summary: gatewayapi_controller: sync.Once prevents retry of GatewayClass field indexer
Description: The gatewayapi_controller uses sync.Once to add a GatewayClass field indexer and start dependent controllers during its first successful reconcile. If the GatewayClass CRD is not yet fully registered with the API server at that point, the IndexField call fails. Because sync.Once never retries, the indexer is permanently broken for the lifetime of the process.

The status_controller depends on this indexer to list GatewayClass resources via client.MatchingFields. When the indexer is missing, every status reconcile fails, and the ingress ClusterOperator never reports its Available/Progressing/Degraded conditions.

### Root Cause

In pkg/operator/controller/gatewayapi/controller.go, the Reconcile method wraps the indexer setup in sync.Once.Do:

1. sync.Once.Do runs exactly once — if IndexField fails, there is no retry path.
2. Dependent controllers (including status_controller) are started even when the indexer fails, so they run with a broken index.

### Impact

The ingress ClusterOperat

Always - on metal upgrade jobs (both minor and micro upgrade variants)

Bug Key: OCPBUGS-78211
Component: OLM
Priority: Normal
Issue Type: Bug
Summary: Boxcutter applier preflight check fails to validate namespace-scoped CREATE permissions, causing installation failures after preflight passes
Description: Description of problem:

````java
After PR #2539 (https://github.com/operator-framework/operator-controller/pull/2539) was merged into downstream with PR 659, the Boxcutter applier's preflight permission check is missing the configuration for namespace-scoped `create` verbs. **Note**: Boxcutter does **not** need `WithClusterCollectionVerbs("list", "watch")`

Always - on metal upgrade jobs (both minor and micro upgrade variants)

Bug Key: OCPBUGS-77958
Component: OLM
Priority: Critical
Issue Type: Bug
Summary: Update NetworkPolicy egress in OLM to support  HyperShift custom API ports
Description: Description of problem:

```java
The current NetworkPolicy rules in operator-lifecycle-manager (OLM v0) hardcode port 6443 for Kubernetes API Server access and have restrictive egress rules for DNS. This breaks HyperShift deployments that customize the API server port via configuration options.

Current State: NetworkPolicy configurations in both static manifests and Go code hardcode port 6443 for kube-apiserver egress, which prevents OLM components from communicating with HyperShift clusters using custom API server ports.


Expected Behavior  Per latest guidance, NetworkPolicy egress rules should:
  1. kube-apiserver: Use wildcard to allow all IPs/Ports ({})
  2. DNS: Use wildcard to allow all IPs with ports 53 and 5353 (TCP and UDP)  This approach is already used by related projects like catalogd and operator-controller.  

Related Work:  This is the OLM v0 counterpart to OCPBUGS-66980, which addresses the same issue in operator-marketplace (static manifests).
```

Summary: OLMv1 test operator httpd binds to IPv4 only, causing 0% pass rate on metal/ipv6/techpreview
Description: ### Summary

The OLMv1 e2e test operator's httpd script uses `python3 -m http.server 8081` which binds to `0.0.0.0` (IPv4 only) by default. On IPv6-only metal clusters, the pod's startup/liveness/readiness probes connect to the pod's IPv6 address (e.g. `[fd01:0:0:6::530]:8081`) but nothing is listening, so the operator pod never becomes Ready.

This causes two blocking OLMv1 tests to fail on every run:

* `[sig-olmv1][OCPFeatureGate:NewOLM] OLMv1 operator installation should install a cluster extension`
* `[sig-olmv1][OCPFeatureGate:NewOLM] OLMv1 operator installation should block cluster upgrades if an incompatible operator is installed`

### Impact

**0% pass rate** (all-time, 58 runs since 2026-02-09) on:

* `periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-4.22-e2e-metal-ipi-ovn-ipv6-techpreview`

The same tests pass at 98.83% (1856/1878) across all other variants, confirming this is isolated to IPv6-only networks.

Bug Key: OCPBUGS-77712
Component: OLM
Priority: Critical
Issue Type: Bug
Summary: Update NetworkPolicy egress in OLM to support  HyperShift custom API ports
Description: Description of problem:

```java
The current NetworkPolicy rules in operator-lifecycle-manager (OLM v0) hardcode port 6443 for Kubernetes API Server access and have restrictive egress rules for DNS. This breaks HyperShift deployments that customize the API server port via configuration options.

Current State: NetworkPolicy configurations in both static manifests and Go code hardcode port 6443 for kube-apiserver egress, which prevents OLM components from communicating with HyperShift clusters using custom API server ports.


Expected Behavior  Per latest guidance, NetworkPolicy egress rules should:
  1. kube-apiserver: Use wildcard to allow all IPs/Ports ({})
  2. DNS: Use wildcard to allow all IPs with ports 53 and 5353 (TCP and UDP)  This approach is already used by related projects like catalogd and operator-controller.  

Related Work:  This is the OLM v0 counterpart to OCPBUGS-66980, which addresses the same issue in operator-marketplace (static manifests).
```

Bug Key: OCPBUGS-77412
Component: Networking / router
Priority: Undefined
Issue Type: Bug
Summary: Changes on routes cause small disruption on DCM (smaller fix)
Description: Description of problem:

```java
Whenever a route is changed, the routing configuration is fully removed and added again on two distinct steps. Although this works well on the reload strategy, it is not the same for DCM because those two operations happen dynamically, by removing and adding the route map entry even if there is no change in the pattern used in the map.

This behavior can potentially cause some disruption on busy environments, and should lead to a short outage of the route in the case the API call to add the route back fails and a reload need to be issued.
```

Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):

```java
-
```

How reproducible:

```java
Always
```

Steps to Reproduce:

```java
* Enable DCM
* Ensure log level is at least 4
* Create a route
* Change the route to a distinct weight
```

Actual results:

```java
Logs include "del map ..." having the map entry ID of the changed route, followed by "add map ..." adding the changed route again.
``

Bug Key: OCPBUGS-77530
Component: HyperShift
Priority: Critical
Issue Type: Bug
Summary: HyperShift nodepool controller deletion path does not clear paused state set by OADP backup
Description: Description of problem:

````java
When OADP backup pauses a HC that is currently being uninstalled, the backup may fail and oadp-recovery unpauses the HC. However, the HyeprShift Operator does not propagate the unpause to the inner layers (i.e. HCP object, Machine/machineDeployment objects).

This looks to be a race in the nodepool controller; Reconcile() enters the deletion branch early when DeletionTimestamp (~L201) is set, thus returning before it can reach the pause/unpause logic (~L379).

This blocks cluster uninstallation, causing the cluster to end up in an error state:
```
State:                          error
Details:                        Manifest work deletion is stuck
```

Impact: stuck uninstallations on ROSA HCP production. This is a race condition which only happens when a cluster is being backed up with paused state set during deletion.
````

```java
n/a
```

100% when r

Summary: Add a note to the Communication Matrix documentation about EndpointSlice limitations and host-open-ports usage
Description: We would like to add the following note to the documentation for [commatrix plugin](https://docs.redhat.com/en/documentation/openshift_container_platform/4.21/html/installation_configuration/configuring-firewall#network-commatrix-plugin-install_configuring-firewall):  
**Note:** The communication matrix relies on EndpointSlices to discover exposed ports. EndpointSlices are automatically created by Kubernetes for each Service. For a full picture of the cluster’s listening ports (as seen via `ss`), please use the `--host-open-ports` flag to capture the actual open ports on the nodes. Bug Key: OCPBUGS-77521
Component: Unknown
Priority: Major
Issue Type: Bug
Summary: Certificate expiry dates are not uniform
Description: Description of problem:

```java
Different certificates in MicroShift have different expiry dates. Some of them have 1y or 10y validity, and having drift in the year is ok. 
What may be disruptive is having different months and/or days in the expiry dates, which would trigger different forced restarts for renewal. It would be ideal to align the expiry date of all certificates to the same day and time.
```

Bug Key: OCPBUGS-77507
Component: HyperShift
Priority: Undefined
Issue Type: Bug
Summary: HCP request-serving reconciliation behavior during single-AZ outage (mec1-az2) - validation of multi-AZ self-healing
Description: During an AWS power outage impacting a single Availability Zone (me-central-1, mec1-az2), we observed degraded behavior in clusters with multi-AZ configuration.

* External trigger: AWS AZ outage (mec1-az2)

* API degradation observed (AssociateAddress / networking errors)

* ROSA Classic and HCP clusters impacted

**Expected Behaviors: For multi-AZ clusters:**

* ROSA Classic: workers are AZ-bound via MachineSets; recreation expected within same AZ.

* ROSA HCP: request-serving nodes are expected to recreate in healthy AZs to preserve serving capacity.

**Observed Behavior:**

* HCP request-serving nodes did not immediately rebalance to healthy AZs.

* Machine reconciliation observed (Provisioning / Deleting states), but replacement progress appeared stalled during outage.

* Need to validate:

    * Whether MachineHealthCheck triggered deletion as expected.
    * Whether replacement machine creation progressed in healthy AZs.
    * Whether AWS API errors were the primary bottleneck.

Bug Key: OCPBUGS-77503
Component: HyperShift
Priority: Undefined
Issue Type: Bug
Summary: Windows ImageType e2e test missing EC2 AMI verification for WinLI billing compliance
Description: ## Description of problem

The Windows ImageType e2e test in HyperShift did not verify that EC2 instances actually use the Windows License Included (WinLI) AMI. Without this critical verification layer, nodes can boot with an incorrect AMI while the test still passes, leading to AWS billing compliance violations where customers are billed incorrectly for Windows licensing.

The test only validated the NodePool condition (`ValidPlatformImageType`) and node OS metadata, but never queried the AWS EC2 API to confirm the actual AMI in use on running instances.

## Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable)

4.21

## How reproducible

Always (structural gap present in all test runs prior to fix)

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Run the Windows ImageType e2e test against a ROSA HCP cluster on a release prior to the fix.
2. Observe that the test validates the `ValidPlatformImageType` NodePool condition is populated.
3. Observe that the test validates node OS is reported as RHCOS/Li

Bug Key: OCPBUGS-77501
Component: Observability UI
Priority: Undefined
Issue Type: Bug
Summary: CVE-2026-25639 openshift4/ose-monitoring-plugin-rhel9: Axios affected by Denial of Service via __proto__ Key in mergeConfig [openshift-4.22]
Description: Security Tracking Issue

Do not make this issue public.

Flaw:

---

Axios affected by Denial of Service via \__proto_\_ Key in mergeConfig

Axios is a promise based HTTP client for the browser and Node.js. Prior to 1.13.5, the mergeConfig function in axios crashes with a TypeError when processing configuration objects containing \__proto_\_ as an own property. An attacker can trigger this by providing a malicious configuration object created via JSON.parse(), causing complete denial of service. This vulnerability is fixed in 1.13.5.

Priority: Undefined
Issue Type: Bug
Summary: e2e-aws-ovn-edge-zones CI job fails: TEST_SKIPS regex "internal should be reachable with hairpinning traffic" matches no tests
Description: ## Description of problem

The CI job `pull-ci-openshift-origin-release-4.19-e2e-aws-ovn-edge-zones` fails immediately in the `openshift-e2e-test` step because the `TEST_SKIPS` workaround configured in the `openshift-e2e-aws-edge-zones` workflow no longer matches any test in the filtered test suite.

The workflow file in openshift/release sets:

```java
TEST_SKIPS: internal should be reachable with hairpinning traffic
# FIXME: remove this once the issue is fixed: https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/OCPBUGS-77166
```

The `openshift-e2e-test-commands.sh` script validates that the `TEST_SKIPS` regex matches at least one test before proceeding. When no tests match, the step exits with code 1 before any real tests run.

This failure is a CI infrastructure issue in the openshift/release step registry - it is unrelated to the PR under test.

Description: AWS is changing default behavior for S3 buckets (with customer managed keys). We need to check whether these changes will affect us and if action is needed. 

Specifically, we need to check whether we allow customer managed keys to encrypt bootstrap ignition and whether this change affects those.

Here's the relevant email, which includes links with more info:

 

Starting on April 6, 2026, we will be changing how [server-side encryption with customer-provided keys (SSE-C)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/ServerSideEncryptionCustomerKeys.html) is enabled for [Amazon S3](https://aws.amazon.com/s3/) buckets. With this change, SSE-C will be disabled by default on all new S3 general purpose buckets. Furthermore, SSE-C will also be disabled for all existing buckets in Amazon Web Services (AWS) Accounts that do not have any SSE-C encrypted data. At this stage, the \`install-config.yaml\` file no longer exists, causing confusion and command failure. Bug Key: OCPBUGS-77480
Component: Networking / ptp
Priority: Major
Issue Type: Bug
Summary: PTP Operator 4.20: ubxtool EnableNMEA/DisableBinary logs success but GNSS remains in binary-only mode unless additional flags (-v 1 -w 2) are explicitly provided
Description: Customer is testing PTP Operator on OpenShift Container Platform 4.20.4 (fresh installation) using Intel E810 NIC with ZED-F9T GNSS module in T-GM configuration.

The linuxptp-daemon internally executes ubxtool commands to enable NMEA and disable BINARY mode. Operator logs show successful execution:

Enable NMEA  
disable binary

No "error executing ubxtool command" messages are present.

However, despite success logs, the GNSS module sometimes remains in binary-only mode. When this occurs, ts2phc fails with:

nmea: no valid rmc fix  
source ts not valid

Customer confirmed that when explicitly defining the same ubxtool commands in PtpConfig under ublxCmds, including additional flags (-v 1 -w 2), the GNSS mode switches correctly and time synchronization works reliably.

This suggests the internal ubxtool invocation may be missing required flags or there may be a timing/race condition during initialization.

Bug Key: OCPBUGS-77459
Component: Security
Priority: Major
Issue Type: Bug
Summary:  RHACS is keeping stale/outdated nodes and resources.
Description: Description of problem:

```java
- After deleting a node, customer found out the node still shows up in the Nodes CVEs page (/main/vulnerabilities/node-cves/nodes/) even if the node was recycled 10 days ago.
- It seems that RHACS is keeping stale/outdated nodes (and resources), and this node should have been removed from RHACS since it does not exist anymore.
- It seems ACS doesn't purge older information.
- Outdated deployments are still showing up in Platfrom Vulnerabilties.
- This leads to false positives and creates unnecessary noise in vulnerability reports/dashboards.
```

How reproducible:

```java
In RHACS 4.8, delete the node with few deployments. After that the node still shows up in the Nodes CVEs page.
```

Actual results:

```java
Node and Deployments still shows up
```

Expected results:

```java
Node and Outdated Deployments should be deleted
```

gRPC-Go: Authorization bypass due to improper HTTP/2 path validation

gRPC-Go is the Go language implementation of gRPC. Versions prior to 1.79.3 have an authorization bypass resulting from improper input validation of the HTTP/2 \`:path\` pseudo-header. The gRPC-Go server was too lenient in its routing logic, accepting requests where the \`:path\` omitted the mandatory leading slash (e.g., \`Service/Method\` instead of \`/Service/Method\`). While the server successfully routed these requests to the correct handler, authorization interceptors (including the official \`grpc/authz\` package) evaluated the raw, non-canonical path string. Consequently, "deny" rules defined using canonical paths (starting with \`/\`) failed to match the incoming request, allowing it to bypass the policy if a fallback "allow" rule was present. This affects gRPC-Go servers that use path-based authorization interceptors, such as the official

```

oc adm must-gather \
  --image=registry.redhat.io/multicluster-engine/must-gather-rhel9:v<mce_version> \
  /usr/bin/gather hosted-cluster-namespace=HOSTEDCLUSTERNAMESPACE \
  hosted-cluster-name=HOSTEDCLUSTERNAME \
  --dest-dir=NAME ; tar -cvzf NAME.tgz NAME

For OCP 4.22, this need to be updated to 

oc adm must-gather \
  --image=registry.redhat.io/rhacm2/acm-must-gather-rhel9:v2.17 \
  /usr/bin/gather hosted-cluster-namespace=HOSTEDCLUSTERNAMESPACE \
  hosted-cluster-name=HOSTEDCLUSTERNAME \
  --dest-dir=NAME ; tar -cvzf NAME.tgz NAME
  
For OCP 4.21, this need to be updated to 

oc adm must-gather \
  --image=registry.redhat.io/rhacm2/acm-must-gather-rhel9:v2.16 \

  hosted-cluster-name=HOSTEDCLUSTERNAME \

Summary: Start-up race between OVS and Corosync can cause unnecessary fencing
Description: # Description of Problem

**CONFIRMED in Run 2 with precise timestamps. Mechanism corrected via adversarial review (2026-04-01).**

On reboot after fencing, `configure-ovs.sh` creates `br-ex` and moves `enp2s0` from standalone NIC to OVS port. This strips the corosync address (`fd2e:..::14`) from `enp2s0`. The address must be re-acquired via DHCPv6 on the new `br-ex` interface. During the \~1.6s gap, knet detects the link as down (within 84ms of the interface state change), the 3000ms TOTEM token timeout fires, and the surviving node fences master-0 again — creating a reboot loop.

**Previous description was wrong**: the CLAUDE.md previously said "`del-br br-ex` kills the corosync NIC." In fact, `br-ex` does NOT exist during the reboot cycle — `del-br` is a no-op (`--if-exists`). The disruptive operation is bridge **creation**, not deletion.

## Boot-order race

1. Boot: corosync starts, knet link comes up on `enp2s0` (standalone NIC with `::14`), node joins cluster
2. `configure-ovs.s

While the server successfully routed these requests to the correct handler, authorization interceptors (including the official \`grpc/authz\` package) evaluated the raw, non-canonical path string. Consequently, "deny" rules defined using canonical paths (starting with \`/\`) failed to match the incoming request, allowing it to bypass the policy if a fallback "allow" rule was present. Bug Key: OCPBUGS-81564
Component: RHCOS
Priority: Undefined
Issue Type: Vulnerability
Summary: CVE-2026-35092 rhcos: Corosync: Denial of Service via integer overflow in join message validation [openshift-4.19]
Description: Security Tracking Issue

Do not make this issue public.

Flaw:

---

Corosync: Denial of Service via integer overflow in join message validation

Integer overflow (wraparound) vulnerability in Corosync’s join message sanity validation in exec/totemsrp.c. The flaw is in check_memb_join_sanity(), where proc_list_entries and failed_list_entries are attacker-controlled 32-bit unsigned values received from the network and are added together before being promoted to size_t. This allows the addition (proc_list_entries + failed_list_entries) to wrap around in 32-bit arithmetic (e.g., 0x80000000 + 0x80000000 = 0), causing required_len to be calculated too small and allowing a short packet to pass validation. As a result, Corosync proceeds with processing malformed input that should have been rejected, which can be exploited remotely without authentication in totemudp/totemudpu mode via crafted UDP packets to crash the service, resulting in a denial of service.

Bug Key: OCPBUGS-81563
Component: RHCOS
Priority: Undefined
Issue Type: Vulnerability
Summary: CVE-2026-35091 rhcos: Corosync: Denial of Service and information disclosure via crafted UDP packet [openshift-4.19]
Description: Security Tracking Issue

Do not make this issue public.

Flaw:

---

Corosync: Denial of Service and information disclosure via crafted UDP packet

Wrong return value vulnerability in the Corosync membership commit token sanity check in exec/totemsrp.c. The flaw occurs in check_memb_commit_token_sanity() where truncated messages (msg_len < sizeof(struct memb_commit_token)) incorrectly return 0 (success) instead of -1 (failure). As a result, message_handler_memb_commit_token() continues processing attacker-controlled, undersized input, performs an allocation based on the short length, and then accesses struct memb_commit_token fields beyond the allocated region, triggering an out-of-bounds read (ASAN-confirmed). This can be exploited remotely without authentication in totemudp/totemudpu mode by sending a single crafted UDP packet to the Corosync port (default 5405), causing a denial of service and potentially leaking limited memory contents.

Bug Key: OCPBUGS-81559
Component: RHCOS
Priority: Critical
Issue Type: Bug
Summary: RHCOS fails to boot with Secure Boot enabled due to kernel-5.14.0-570.105.1.el9_6 signed with new CA8 certificate
Description: ## Summary

RHCOS builds using kernel-5.14.0-570.105.1.el9_6 fail to boot when UEFI Secure Boot is enabled. This is blocking nightlies for OCP 4.19, 4.20, and 4.21.

The kernel is signed with a new certificate (Red Hat Secure Boot CA 8 / cert 801) instead of the previous certificate (Red Hat Secure Boot CA 5 / cert 501). This change was not coordinated with OpenShift/RHCOS.

## Impact

* **Nightlies blocked**: OCP 4.19/4.20/4.21 nightlies have been stalled for 2+ days
* **driver-toolkit mismatch**: driver-toolkit was built with kernel-570.105.1, but RHCOS cannot use this kernel, causing FAILED_CONSISTENCY_REQUIREMENT violations
* **Potential cluster bricking**: If this kernel were shipped, existing clusters with Secure Boot enabled could fail to boot after update

## Root Cause

The RHEL kernel team changed the Secure Boot signing certificate from CA5 (501) to CA8 (801) starting with kernel-5.14.0-570.105.1.el9_6 as part of migration from 2K to 4K certificates ([RHEL-129223](https://re

Description: ## Summary

The `fips-mode-setup --check` command is no longer available in RHCOS 10 (based on RHEL 10). Documentation needs to be updated to reflect the new method for checking FIPS mode status.

## Background

The `fips-mode-setup` tool has been removed from RHEL 10 as per \[RHEL-65652|[https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/RHEL-65652](https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/RHEL-65652)\]. Users attempting to use the previous method will encounter an error:

```java
$ oc debug node/$node\_name \-\- chroot /host fips\-mode\-setup \-\-check
chroot: failed to run command fips\-mode\-setup : No such file or directory
```

## Proposed Documentation Update

Add the following information to the RHCOS 10 documentation:

_Checking FIPS Mode Status in RHCOS 10_

In RHCOS 10, the `fips-mode-setup` tool is no longer available. To check if FIPS mode is enabled, verify the contents of `/proc/sys/crypto/fips_enabled`:

```java
$ oc debug node/$node\_name \-\- chroot /host cat /proc/sys/crypto/fips\_enabl

Component: Bare Metal Hardware Provisioning / baremetal-operator
Priority: Undefined
Issue Type: Bug
Summary: ironic-proxy DaemonSet pods enter CrashLoopBackOff due to Read-only filesystem; need to enforce privileged SCC via annotation.
Description: **Description of problem:**

```
After upgrading to OpenShift 4.20.8, ironic-proxy DaemonSet pods in the openshift-machine-api namespace enter CrashLoopBackOff with "read-only file system" errors.

The pods are assigned a non-default SCC (pure-csi-node-plugin-scc) created by the Portworx Enterprise Operator, which enforces a read-only root filesystem. This conflicts with ironic-proxy requirements and prevents normal operation.

Adding the openshift.io/required-scc: privileged annotation ensures the correct SCC is used and resolves the issue.
```

**Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):**

```
‌
```

**How reproducible:**

```
Always
```

**Steps to Reproduce:**

```
1. Deploy an OpenShift cluster.
2. Install the Portworx Enterprise Operator which introduces the pure-csi-node-plugin-scc.
3. Observe the status of the ironic-proxy pods in the openshift-machine-api namespace.
```

**Actual results:**

```
1. ironic-proxy pods are in CrashLoopBackOff.
2. Pod metadata

Bug Key: OCPBUGS-81546
Component: HyperShift
Priority: Normal
Issue Type: Bug
Summary: AWSEndpointService can have duplicate SubnetIDs causing AWS VPC Endpoint reconciliation failures
Description: ## Problem

 The `AWSEndpointService` CRD's `Spec.SubnetIDs` field lacks a `+listType=set` marker, so the API server does not enforce uniqueness. If duplicate subnet IDs are present (from    
  manual edits, older resources, or server-side apply merges), the control-plane-operator passes them directly to AWS `CreateVpcEndpoint` and `ModifyVpcEndpoint` calls, which could fail because AWS rejects duplicate subnet IDs.                                                                                                                                  

## Root Cause

* `api/hypershift/v1beta1/endpointservice_types.go` — `SubnetIDs []string` has no `+listType=set`, so the CRD allows duplicates.                                                
* `control-plane-operator/controllers/awsprivatelink/awsprivatelink_controller.go` — `reconcileAWSEndpointService()` passes `Spec.SubnetIDs` directly to AWS APIs without  
  deduplication. The `diffIDs()` function also propagates duplicates from the desired

Flaw:

Moby: Privilege validation bypass during plugin installation

Moby is an open source container framework. Prior to version 29.3.1, a security vulnerability has been detected that allows plugins privilege validation to be bypassed during docker plugin install. Due to an error in the daemon's privilege comparison logic, the daemon may incorrectly accept a privilege set that differs from the one approved by the user. Plugins that request exactly one privilege are also affected, because no comparison is performed at all. This issue has been patched in version 29.3.1.

Summary: CVE-2026-33997 openshift4/ose-console: Moby: Privilege validation bypass during plugin installation [openshift-4.12.z]
Description: Security Tracking Issue

Do not make this issue public.

Flaw:

---

Moby: Privilege validation bypass during plugin installation

Moby is an open source container framework. Prior to version 29.3.1, a security vulnerability has been detected that allows plugins privilege validation to be bypassed during docker plugin install. Due to an error in the daemon's privilege comparison logic, the daemon may incorrectly accept a privilege set that differs from the one approved by the user. Plugins that request exactly one privilege are also affected, because no comparison is performed at all. This issue has been patched in version 29.3.1.

Flaw:

---

gRPC-Go: Authorization bypass due to improper HTTP/2 path validation

gRPC-Go is the Go language implementation of gRPC. Versions prior to 1.79.3 have an authorization bypass resulting from improper input validation of the HTTP/2 \`:path\` pseudo-header. The gRPC-Go server was too lenient in its routing logic, accepting requests where the \`:path\` omitted the mandatory leading slash (e.g., \`Service/Method\` instead of \`/Service/Method\`). While the server successfully routed these requests to the correct handler, authorization interceptors (including the official \`grpc/authz\` package) evaluated the raw, non-canonical path string. Consequently, "deny" rules defined using canonical paths (starting with \`/\`) failed to match the incoming request, allowing it to bypass the policy if a fallback "allow" rule was present. This affects gRPC-Go servers that use path-based authorization interceptors, such as the official
